title: Repository Compliance Manifest
description: The compliance declaration every platform repository places at its root as .compliance-manifest.yaml. Declares the repository's type, governing profile, technology contexts, and any active waivers. This file is the entry point for all compliance enforcement.

$schema.description: Schema reference for editor tooling. Not validated against the schema itself.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema used to produce the manifest.

url.description: Full HTTPS URL to the repository (e.g., 'https://github.com/org/repo').

type.description: Repository type from 02-taxonomy/repository-types.yaml. Determines which scope conditions apply.

has_container_images.description: Declares whether this repository builds or references Docker container images. Activates RUN and SUP-002 controls.

service.description: Present when type is 'service'. Provides service-specific metadata.

externally_exposed.description: Whether the service is accessible from outside the private network. Activates NET-001.

declared_profiles.description: Compliance profile IDs from 04-profiles/. At least one required. The listed profiles drive all gate checks.

environment.description: Committed, governed deployment-environment declaration used by SEC-013 (TLS enforcement) and other environment-sensitive controls. This is the source of truth for the repository's environment, replacing the gitignored terraform.tfvars (which CI cannot read, so it always defaulted to 'staging' and masked production). Fail-safe semantics: this field is OPTIONAL, but when it is ABSENT on a terraform-root repository, tooling MUST treat the environment as 'production' (TLS is enforced unless a repo explicitly declares 'staging' or 'development'). Absence therefore defaults to the secure behavior.

technology_contexts.description: Technology contexts from 02-taxonomy/technology-contexts.yaml. Determines which bindings and policies apply.

waiver_ids.description: Active waiver IDs from 09-assessments/waivers/. Referenced here to make active waivers visible at the manifest level.

compliance_contact.description: Team or individual responsible for this repository's compliance state. Receives compliance failure notifications.

last_updated.description: Date the manifest was last reviewed or updated (ISO 8601).
